member.name: T:CommonRemarks.Async_Warning
member.remarks:
⚠️ This method runs "pseudo-async": it does not create real OS-managed asynchronous threads. Instead, operations are executed on a single background thread or a limited thread pool.
Technical details: - Tasks are executed sequentially or via a SemaphoreSlim-limited concurrency. - The method uses async/await only to avoid blocking the caller thread. - Long-running operations may still block system resources if overused. - For true OS-level async I/O, additional API or native async file operations are required.
Deletes folders according to filters. ⚠️ Async-Like Methods Notice: This method uses TaskManager to limit concurrency and is not a true async/parallel operation. You can control the maximum number of threads with one of these TaskManager methods: - NeraTools.TaskManager.SetThreadsCount_ByPercent(ThreadUsagePercent percent) - NeraTools.TaskManager.SetThreadsCount_ByCore(int coreCount) - NeraTools.TaskManager.SetThreadsCount_ByThreads(int threadCount)
Important: - Thread limits apply to this type of async-like operations only and must be set before calling them. - Setting too many threads may reduce performance; too few may slow down execution.
